briefdescription.para: Default constructor, initializes the vector to 0. @SINCE_1_0.0.

memberdef.argsstring: (float x, float y, float z, float w)
memberdef.name: Vector4

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
x
x (or r/s) component
y
y (or g/t) component
z
z (or b/p) component
w
w (or a/q) component

briefdescription.para: Conversion constructor from an array of four floats.
Conversion constructor from Vector3 and float.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_4.19
vec3
Vector3 to copy from
w
w (or a/q) component

@SINCE_1_0.0
array
Array of floats
Itself

Only sets x and y. z and w are left as they were. @SINCE_1_0.0
vec2
A reference to assign from
Itself

Only sets x and y and z. w is left as it was. @SINCE_1_0.0
vec3
A reference to assign from
Itself

rhs
Vector to add

The negative value
Utilizes appropriate machine epsilon values.

@SINCE_1_0.0
rhs
The vector to test against
True if the vectors are equal
Utilizes appropriate machine epsilon values.

@SINCE_1_0.0
rhs
The vector to test against
True if the vectors are not equal

Asserts if index is out of range. Should be 0, 1, 2 or 3. @SINCE_1_0.0
index

The float at the given index

Asserts if index is out of range. Should be 0, 1, 2 or 3. @SINCE_1_0.0
index
Subscript index
The float at the given index

briefdescription.para: Returns the dot product of this vector (4d) and another vector (3d).

detaileddescription.para:
The dot product is the length of one vector in the direction of another vector. This is great for lighting, threshold testing the angle between two unit vectors, calculating the distance between two points in a particular direction. @SINCE_1_0.0
other
The other vector
The dot product

briefdescription.para: Returns the dot product of this vector and another vector.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
It will consider w parameter After 2.4.19
other
The other vector
The dot product

briefdescription.para: Returns the dot product of this vector and another vector.

detaileddescription.para:
The dot product is the length of one vector in the direction of another vector. This is great for lighting, threshold testing the angle between two unit vectors, calculating the distance between two points in a particular direction. @SINCE_2_4.19
other
The other vector
The dot product

briefdescription.para: Returns the 4d dot product of this vector and another vector.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0 @DEPRECATED_2_4.19 Use Dot instead.
other
The other vector
The dot product

briefdescription.para: Returns the cross produce of this vector and another vector.

detaileddescription.para: The cross produce of two vectors is a vector which is perpendicular to the plane of the two vectors. This is great for calculating normals and making matrices orthogonal.

@SINCE_1_0.0
other
The other vector
A vector containing the cross product

briefdescription.para: Returns the length of the vector.
Returns the length of the xyz components of vector.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_4.19
The length

briefdescription.para: Returns the length of the vector squared.

detaileddescription.para:
This is faster than using Length() when performing threshold checks as it avoids use of the square root. @SINCE_1_0.0
The length of the vector squared

briefdescription.para: Returns the length of the xyz components of vector squared.

detaileddescription.para:
This is faster than using Length3() when performing threshold checks as it avoids use of the square root. @SINCE_2_4.19
The length of the vector squared

Sets the vector to unit length whilst maintaining its direction. @SINCE_1_0.0
It will consider w parameter After 2.4.19

briefdescription.para: Normalizes the xyz components of vector. w component not be changed.

detaileddescription.para: Sets the vector to unit length whilst maintaining its direction. @SINCE_2_4.19

briefdescription.para: Clamps the vector between minimum and maximum vectors.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
min
The minimum vector
max
The maximum vector

briefdescription.para: Returns the contents of the vector as an array of 4 floats.
Returns the contents of the vector as an array of 4 floats.

detaileddescription.para: Components can be used as position or offset (x,y,z,w); color (r,g,b,a) or texture coords(s,t,p,q). @SINCE_1_0.0
